generated_comment: Arre yaar, these boys say those mother-sister type gaalis for everything now, but it is not like they literally mean any woman. It is just a way of talking in anger, bas. People make it a gender issue unnecessarily.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes abuse rooted in women's bodies and family relations by dismissing it as harmless expression. It encodes the 'it's just a word' defense and lack of recognition of the misogynistic structure of common slang.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 49. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your daughter Sana who is 25 has been working at a private company for two years and is doing well. A proposal has come for her from a good family. The boy is 28, settled, working in the Gulf. His family has said that they expect the wife to be at home after marriage Ñ they are comfortable financially and do not need her income. Sana has said she does not want to stop working. You are sitting with your wife discussing what to say to Sana and whether this proposal Ñ which ticks every other box Ñ should be declined because of this one issue or whether Sana needs to understand what she would be giving up by holding onto this one condition.
